steps.m: to press or squeeze out essence
time/era (from the idea of squeezing out moments)
steps.n: The root meaning of squeezing evolved to mean time/era, like how we 'squeeze' the most out of our time. This pattern makes nouns from verbs.
Adds 'and' to connect this word to what comes before it
Adding ال makes it refer to a specific time/era, not just any time
The ِ ending shows this word is affected by the oath word و at the start. The ا in ال becomes ٱ after و.
This is a special word that starts sentences to make them stronger. It's like saying 'Indeed' or 'Truly' in English
Adding the shadda makes the word stronger and connects it to the word after it (ٱلْإِنسٰنَ meaning 'mankind'). It's like saying 'Truly, mankind...'
The root letters give the basic idea of being social or friendly
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb
Changes from a verb about being social into a noun meaning 'human' - showing how humans got their name from being social creatures
Added ال to make it specific (the human) and changed alif to alif with bar (ٰ) for writing style
Added fatha at the end to show this is the object of إِنَّ (indeed/verily) in the verse
This is a basic preposition that shows something is inside or within something else. In this ayah, it shows that mankind is 'in' a state of loss (خُسْرٍ).
The لَ at the start adds emphasis, showing this is definitely true. It works with إِنَّ at the start of the ayah to make the meaning very strong and certain.

steps.m: to lose or be at a loss
steps.n: The root gives the basic idea of losing or being at a loss
Changed the word pattern to show the concept of loss itself rather than the action of losing

steps.m: in a state of loss (after لَفِى)
steps.n: Added the tanween kasra (ٍ) to show this word comes after the word فِى (in) in the phrase لَفِى (surely in)
This is the basic form of the exception particle that shows something is being excluded from what was mentioned before
The shadda and alif are added to make the exception stronger and clearer. In this ayah, it excludes the believers (ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟) from those who are in loss.

steps.m: those who - points to specific people or things being described
steps.n: This is a relative pronoun used to describe a group of people. The ين ending shows it's plural

steps.m: to trust, believe in, feel secure with

steps.n: Basic root letters أ-م-ن got short 'a' vowels (فتحة) to make the past tense verb 'he believed'
The long alif (آ) at start changes it from 'believe' to 'make someone believe'
Changed from 'he believed' to 'they believed' by adding وا at the end to show many people, and the ء at start shows strong belief
Connected the root letters with vowels to make a past tense verb
Adds وا to make plural past tense 'they did'

steps.m: and they did/worked (connected to ءَامَنُوا - believed)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this action with the previous word ءَامَنُوا (believed), showing these people both believed AND did good works
Connected the root letters with fatha to make a basic past tense verb
Changed from verb (being good) to noun (person who is good)
Adds the feminine plural ending to make it refer to multiple righteous things/deeds
Added ٱل to make it specific and ِ to show it's the object of عَمِلُوا۟ (they did/performed)
Connected the root letters and added vowels to make the basic past tense verb. The final ي changes to ى because it's a weak letter at the end.
This pattern shows people doing the action to each other. The word is in nominative case as part of a series of verbs describing what the believers do.

steps.m: they advised one another (connected to ءَامَنُوا and عَمِلُوا)
steps.n: Adding وْا shows that 'they' (the believers) did this action. This verb is connected to the previous verbs ءَامَنُوا (they believed) and عَمِلُوا (they did/worked).
Adding conjunction 'and' to connect to previous phrase
The doubled letter (shadda) shows this is a noun meaning 'truth' rather than the action of 'being truthful'
Adding ال makes it specific (the truth), and the ِ shows it follows the word بِ (with/by)
The بِ at the beginning shows how the action تَوَاصَوْا (they advised each other) was done - with/by the truth
Added long alif after first root letter to show mutual action between people
Added تَ at beginning to show people doing action together
Added وا at the end to show a group of people did this action

steps.m: and they advised one another (connected to ءَامَنُوا۟ and عَمِلُوا۟)
steps.n: Added و at beginning to connect this action with the previous actions in the verse (believing and doing good deeds)
Changed the root into a noun that names the quality of being patient
Adding 'al' makes the word definite. The first letter gets doubled (shadda) because it comes after 'al'
Adding preposition بِ to indicate 'with/by'
The small i-sound (ِ) was added at the end because this word is connected to تَوَاصَوْا (they advised one another with patience)
